Event: Chris comforts a victim while murdering them
Explanation: He maintains politeness to keep killings efficient, aiding his work while giving no benefit and clear lethal harm to the victim.

Event: Chris speaks against Marlo’s decision to kill Bodie
Explanation: His brief objection risks displeasing his boss with no gain while (slightly) trying to protect Bodie’s life.

Event: Chris speaks against Marlo’s later decision to kill Michael
Explanation: Again he mildly resists an order to kill Michael, risking status while attempting to spare his protégé.

Event: Chris reacts unhappily to news that Omar was killed by a random kid
Explanation: He gains nothing and loses a respected worthy opponent, while Omar is already dead so no direct benefit or harm to others occurs.

Event: Chris and Snoop bicker about how executions should be carried out
Explanation: The disagreement marginally serves Chris’s preferences in methods while only creating minor friction with Snoop.

Event: Chris beats Michael’s stepfather to death for being a child molester
Explanation: The uncontrolled rage killing exposes him to legal risk and capture while removing a predator but in a way that backfires on him and their crew.

Event: Chris trains his soldiers to shoot for the head or groin
Explanation: He increases his and his crew’s combat effectiveness while making them deadlier to others.

Event: Chris disposes of or hides bodies after killings
Explanation: He protects himself and the crew from detection while worsening closure and justice for others.

Event: Chris gives Marlo honest advice and carries out ruthless orders to the letter
Explanation: His loyalty and competence secure his position and influence while enabling Marlo’s violent power.

Event: Chris visits his girlfriend and kids during a gang war
Explanation: He nurtures his family bonds and well-being, briefly improving their emotional life despite some security risk.

Event: Chris later avoids visiting his family to keep them from being targeted
Explanation: He sacrifices personal contact to protect them from violent retribution, benefiting them more than himself.

Event: Chris becomes extremely angry when he fails to kill Omar because it means he can’t go back to his family
Explanation: His frustration harms his composure and relationships without improving his situation or theirs.

Event: Chris goes into hiding during Marlo’s war with Omar to keep his family safe
Explanation: He reduces his ability to act openly but significantly increases his family’s safety.

Event: Chris takes good care of his family even after being imprisoned for life
Explanation: Despite his own lost freedom, he invests resources and care to support his family’s well-being.

Event: Chris paces, throws a knife into the floor, and snaps at Snoop when forced to stay from family
Explanation: His stress behaviors give him no benefit and damage his working relationship without helping anyone.

Event: Chris drags Michael into the drug trade and turns him into a killer while saving him and his brother from an abusive home
Explanation: He gains a loyal young soldier and rescues them from abuse but at the cost of Michael’s corruption and danger.

Event: Chris becomes a father figure to Michael
Explanation: He gains loyalty and emotional connection while giving Michael guidance and protection, despite the criminal context.

Event: Chris is hesitant to believe Michael is a snitch but ultimately does not stop the gang from hunting him
Explanation: His weak resistance neither protects Michael nor clearly benefits Chris, allowing harm with no gain.

Event: Chris and Snoop teach Michael and other “pups” the ways of the game
Explanation: He builds capable subordinates and organizational strength while pushing youths deeper into crime and violence.

Event: Chris, with Marlo’s crew, gives children money for clothes and books before school
Explanation: The gesture boosts Marlo’s image and potential future loyalty while tangibly helping kids with school needs.

Event: Chris tries to convince Marlo that the giveaway will “make his name ring out”
Explanation: He offers branding advice that could improve both Marlo’s power and community perceptions.

Event: Chris kills problems, rivals, and potential leaks on behalf of the organization
Explanation: He solidifies his value and their power structure while severely harming targets and public safety.

Event: Chris tortures Blind Butchie to get Omar’s location
Explanation: The torture serves his mission to draw out Omar while inflicting extreme harm on Butchie.

Event: Chris kills Blind Butchie after commending his refusal to talk
Explanation: He ends the interrogation and uses Butchie’s death as bait for Omar, benefiting the crew while killing an innocent mentor figure.

Event: Chris plans Butchie’s murder so Omar will come out of hiding for revenge
Explanation: He sets an effective trap to kill a dangerous enemy, enhancing his crew’s strategic position while endangering Omar.

Event: Chris tortures a blind old man to death (Butchie) despite respecting him
Explanation: His respect doesn’t change the outcome; he executes a brutal mission that advances his side while destroying the victim.

Event: Chris weaponizes his knowledge of Baltimore club music to identify New York dealers
Explanation: He improves his ability to spot and target outsiders, helping his crew while exposing others to violence.

Event: Chris asks officials in the courthouse for directions without them realizing who he is
Explanation: He safely gathers needed information while leaving them unharmed and unaware.

Event: Chris spits on Michael’s stepfather
Explanation: The spiteful act provides fleeting emotional release, later contributing to his capture while not materially helping anyone.

Event: Chris beats Devar’s face in so violently that he breaks his own protocols, leaving DNA evidence
Explanation: His deviation from clean methods leads to forensic exposure and eventual arrest with no compensating benefit.

Event: Chris visits his girlfriend and kids warmly and affectionately amid the war
Explanation: He strengthens family bonds and gives them emotional comfort while getting personal solace himself.

Event: Chris befriends Wee-Bey in prison
Explanation: He gains a strong ally and companionship inside while mutually supporting another lifer.

Event: Chris commends Butchie’s toughness before killing him
Explanation: The respect offers negligible personal benefit and no real comfort while the lethal outcome still serves his mission.

Event: Chris remains completely loyal and refuses to talk as long as Marlo cares for his family
Explanation: He sacrifices his own chances of freedom to protect his family and uphold loyalty, benefiting them and Marlo at his own expense.
